title: Prior-Product Audit, Maintenance Reality, and Reframed Challenge · Stakeholder Workshop
summary: A guided product team extends maintenance audit and reframed product charter while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of maintenance audit and reframed product charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.
Classify feedback by user outcome, evidence source, frequency, severity, reach, confidence, and strategic fit.
Measure dependency, defect, support, content, data, infrastructure, and cognitive maintenance cost before adding scope.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Production Vs Demo
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A first product has dependency drift, support burden, unresolved accessibility feedback, stale assumptions, and pressure for a feature-rich sequel. The workshop variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Production Vs Demo.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Production Vs Demo, keep Production Vs Demo connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A first product has dependency drift, support burden, unresolved accessibility feedback, stale assumptions, and pressure for a feature-rich sequel. The workshop variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Production Vs Demo condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
